lexical_matching: "Analiza teških metala" in the context of digital humanities refers to a method of analysis performed on artifacts, such as potsherds, to identify the materials used in their creation. This process involves crushing a shard of pottery and placing it into a viscous fluid where heavier minerals sink to the bottom. The purpose of this analysis is to determine the geological source of the sand inclusions in the clay of the pot, which can help identify the probable area of manufacture. The method includes crushing 10-30 grams of pottery and floating the resulting powder on a heavy liquid like bromoform with a specific gravity of 2.85. Heavy minerals such as zircon, garnet, epidote, and tourmaline sink, while quartz sand and clay float. These heavy minerals are then separated, identified, and counted under a low-power microscope to characterize the parent formation and identify the source of the sand.
